brief.headline_zh: 欧SEP司法碎片化与费率冲突加剧
brief.headline_en: European SEP Litigation Intensifies Amid Jurisdictional Conflicts and Arbitration Shifts
brief.summary_zh: 2026年欧洲SEP领域呈现司法管辖权激烈博弈。英国法院确立全球费率裁定权，中英判决出现直接冲突；UPC打破德国传统尝试裁定费率并签发反禁诉令。专利池向车联网及Wi-Fi扩张，许可模式趋向仲裁化。中国企业在欧面临高额判赔及禁令风险，需应对多法域诉讼策略差异。
key_points: 英中法院就三星/中兴案作出冲突的全球FRAND费率判决，凸显司法碎片化。
UPC积极介入费率裁定并签发反禁诉令，限制中国法院临时许可效力。
Avanci等专利池扩展至车联网和Wi-Fi领域，英国推动FRAND争议仲裁化。
actions: 审查许可协议中UPC退出机制及仲裁条款，规避集中无效及管辖被动风险。
建立中欧双轨应诉策略，利用英国程序争取有利全球费率，防范UPC禁令。
评估车联网及Wi-Fi产品专利敞口，提前参与专利池谈判或准备无效证据。
watchlist: 英国最高法院关于专利池FRAND义务及全球管辖权的最终裁决动向。
UPC上诉法院对反禁诉令（AASI）及跨境管辖权界限的后续判例。
欧盟SEP新规立法进程及英国IPO监管框架改革的具体实施细则。

sep_ai_analysis.summary_zh: SEP 长窗从 2026.03 累计到现在，共识别 109 条高相关信号。近120天里，许可动作占比最高，欧洲层面仍是最活跃场域。
sep_ai_analysis.summary_en: The SEP long window now spans from Mar 2026 to the present with 109 high-relevance signals. In the recent 120-day window, Licensing lead the mix and Europe-wide remains the busiest forum.

insight_cards.insight_zh: 许可动作已经从背景变量变成当前主线，说明专利池扩围、费率博弈和双边谈判正在持续抬高经营准备门槛。
insight_cards.insight_en: Licensing has moved from a background factor to the main storyline, showing pools, rate-setting, and bilateral talks are raising the preparation bar.
insight_cards.action_zh: 优先把许可覆盖、费率对标和 willing licensee 证据准备前置。
insight_cards.action_en: Front-load licence coverage checks, rate benchmarking, and willing-licensee evidence.
insight_cards.evidence_zh: 近120天 许可动作 63 / 前120天 0 / 长窗累计 63

insight_cards.insight_zh: 欧洲层面是近120天最活跃的场域，英国紧随其后，争议正在形成多法域联动。
insight_cards.insight_en: Europe-wide is the busiest forum in the recent window, with United Kingdom close behind, pointing to multi-forum spillover.
insight_cards.action_zh: 优先盯住 欧洲层面 的裁判与程序变化，并同步观察外溢到其他法域的连锁影响。
insight_cards.action_en: Track procedural and decisional shifts in Europe-wide first, then watch for spillover into other venues.
insight_cards.evidence_zh: 欧洲层面 62 条；英国 23 条

insight_cards.insight_zh: 近120天中企相关信号共有 37 条，占近期样本 34%。重点企业包括 ZTE / Huawei / Oppo。
insight_cards.insight_en: There are 37 China-related signals in the recent 120-day window, or 34% of the sample. Focus companies include ZTE / Huawei / Oppo.
insight_cards.action_zh: 把许可准备、诉讼预案和客户交付协同放到同一张经营清单里，不要只按单案应对。
insight_cards.action_en: Put licensing readiness, litigation planning, and customer-delivery coordination on the same operating checklist rather than treating them as one-off disputes.

insight_cards.insight_zh: 当前约 96% 的专题样本基于全文或公开预览分析，摘要级样本主要用于补趋势，不宜单独放大解读。
insight_cards.insight_en: About 96% of the topic rests on full-text or public-preview analysis; summary-only items are mainly useful for directional trend support.
insight_cards.action_zh: 对涉及重大经营判断的节点，优先回到原文或公开预览核实，再决定是否升级为重点预警。
insight_cards.action_en: For business-critical signals, step back to the source text or public preview before escalating them into priority warnings.
insight_cards.evidence_zh: 全文 34 / 预览 71 / 摘要 4

当前最强主线仍是许可动作，近120天共 63 条，说明专利池、费率与双边谈判继续主导节奏。
欧洲层面是近120天最活跃的主导法域/场域，共出现 62 条相关信号。
中企相关信号近120天共有 37 条，占近期样本 34%；当前专题中约 96% 的样本基于全文或公开预览分析。

Licensing remains the strongest line with 63 recent signals, keeping pools, rates, and bilateral talks at the center.
Europe-wide is the most active forum in the recent 120-day window with 62 signals.

brief.china_impact_summary_zh: 这条长窗显示，SEP 风险已经从传统通信终端延伸到车载与 IoT，并在德国、英国和 UPC 三条诉讼线同步抬升。对中国企业来说，下一阶段重点不只是单案应对，而是把许可准备、诉讼预案和规则跟踪并行推进。
china_action_points: 涉及车载、蜂窝、Wi-Fi 或 IoT 出海业务的中国企业，应尽快核查专利池与双边许可覆盖，避免产品上市后再被动补谈。
在德国、英国和 UPC 有销售、供应链或客户落点的企业，应同步准备 FRAND 谈判材料、可比许可证据与禁令应对预案。
法务、标准与商务团队需要持续跟踪英欧 SEP 政策和竞争法变化，避免沿用单一市场的许可策略应对欧洲。
针对已进入欧洲市场的中国终端、部件与整车企业，建议把 SEP 许可预算、争议升级路径和管辖博弈纳入年度经营安排。

brief.summary_en: The European SEP landscape in 2026 is defined by significant judicial fragmentation and evolving procedural mechanisms. UK courts have asserted global FRAND rate-setting authority, creating direct conflicts with Asian jurisdictions, while the Unified Patent Court (UPC) actively issues anti-anti-suit injunctions to protect its proceedings. Recent rulings validate arbitration-linked licensing offers as FRAND-compliant, signaling a shift toward alternative dispute resolution. Simultaneously, patent pools are expanding into IoT and Wi-Fi sectors. Implementers face heightened risks of injunctions and conflicting global rates, necessitating coordinated multi-jurisdictional strategies. The UPC’s emerging case law on confidentiality and pleading standards further complicates defense tactics, requiring precise management of licensing negotiations and litigation posture across Europe.
key_points_en: Conflicting global FRAND rulings between UK and Asian courts highlight severe jurisdictional fragmentation and forum-shopping risks.
UPC courts issue anti-anti-suit injunctions against foreign interim licenses, asserting supremacy over parallel proceedings in member states.
UK jurisprudence validates arbitration-based licensing offers, encouraging rights holders to bypass traditional court-determined rate settings.
actions_en: Review license agreements for UPC opt-out clauses and arbitration mandates to mitigate centralized validity attacks and jurisdictional surprises.
Develop dual-track litigation strategies that leverage UK procedural advantages for global rates while safeguarding against UPC injunctions.
Assess patent exposure in IoT and Wi-Fi portfolios early to engage proactively in patent pool negotiations or prepare invalidity defenses.
watchlist_en: UK Supreme Court developments regarding global FRAND jurisdiction and the enforceability of patent pool obligations.
UPC Court of Appeal precedents defining the scope of anti-anti-suit injunctions and cross-border jurisdictional limits.
Implementation details of UKIPO regulatory reforms and EU legislative progress on SEP transparency and dispute resolution.

items.title: President’s mission to Georgia
items.title_zh: EPO局长出访格鲁吉亚深化专利合作
items.title_en: EPO President Visits Georgia to Strengthen Patent Validation and Technology Transfer Cooperation
items.summary: During a mission to Georgia, the EPO President highlighted the role of patents, validation and skills in technology transfer, reinforcing cooperation with Sakpatenti and innovation stakeholders.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月，欧洲专利局（EPO）局长António Campinos访问格鲁吉亚第比利斯，与格鲁吉亚国家知识产权中心（Sakpatenti）及教育部举行高层会晤。双方重申长期伙伴关系，重点讨论专利在技术转让中的作用及格鲁吉亚与欧洲专利实践的接轨。会议强调了验证协议的成功实施，两年内已产生300多项验证请求，有效促进了创新生态系统的建设、外国投资及新技术发展。
items.ai_summary_en: European Patent Office President António Campinos visited Tbilisi in April 2026 to reinforce cooperation with Sakpatenti and Georgian government officials. The mission highlighted the success of the EPO-Georgia validation agreement, which has generated over 300 requests in two years. Discussions with the Minister of Education, Science and Youth focused on aligning Georgian practices with European standards and leveraging patents for economic development. A joint conference explored how patents facilitate technology transfer from research institutions to the market, emphasizing the role of intellectual property in attracting foreign investment and building a competitive innovation ecosystem.
items.ai_insight_en: This mission underscores the EPO’s strategy to extend its geographic reach through validation agreements, offering rights holders a streamlined route into emerging markets. For European applicants, the growing uptake of validation in Georgia signals increasing commercial interest in the region. Monitoring these developments helps portfolio managers assess the value of extending protection to non-EU states aligned with EPO standards, facilitating broader technology transfer and investment opportunities.

items.title: Come Together? Acer v Nokia and the Contractual Turn of FRAND Arbitration
items.title_zh: 汇聚一堂？Acer诉Nokia案与FRAND仲裁的合同化转向
items.title_en: Acer v Nokia: UK Court of Appeal Validates FRAND Arbitration Mechanisms
items.summary: "The readiness is all." - HamletSometimes a judgment does not change the melody of FRAND law. It changes the instrument through which that melody may be played. Not every player reads the same score, and not every performance is equally faithful to the underlying composition. What the UK Court of Appeal has done in Acer v Nokia is reassign one voice in the ensemble - without rewriting the work.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Acer诉Nokia案中裁定，诺基亚提出的“可调整许可要约”（即立即 interim 许可，最终条款由ICC仲裁确定）构成客观上的RAND要约。实施者若拒绝该要约，可能丧失在英国法院寻求竞争性RANDdeclaratory judgment的资格。此案未重写FRAND法律，但改变了其执行工具，确认在特定情形下仲裁可作为履行FRAND承诺的法律形式。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal in Acer v Nokia upheld jurisdiction but granted a stay, recognizing Nokia’s ‘Adjustable Licence Offer’ as an objectively RAND-compliant proposal. This offer provided an immediate interim license with final terms to be determined by ICC arbitration. The court ruled that refusing such an offer may deprive implementers of the right to seek competing declaratory judgments in English courts. While not altering substantive FRAND law, the decision validates arbitration as a legitimate procedural instrument for fulfilling standard-essential patent licensing undertakings, shifting focus from judicial determination to contractual dispute resolution mechanisms in global SEP conflicts.
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling signals a procedural shift in UK SEP litigation, prioritizing contractual arbitration offers over judicial rate-setting. Rights holders can now leverage structured arbitration proposals to mitigate multi-jurisdictional litigation risks. Implementers must carefully evaluate interim offers containing arbitration clauses, as rejection may forfeit favorable forum advantages. European and US counsel should monitor how this contractual approach influences global negotiation dynamics and parallel proceedings in other key jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院认定包含ICC仲裁条款的 interim 许可要约可构成客观RAND要约", "实施者拒绝此类要约可能导致其无法 invoking 英国法院的declaratory…", "判决强调仲裁作为FRAND义务履行形式的合法性，而非重写SDO专利政策"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UK Court of Appeal recognized adjustable license offers with ICC arbitration as objectively RAND-compliant proposals.", "Implementers refusing such arbitration-based offers may lose access to English court declaratory jurisdiction for rate setting.", "Judgment validates arbitration as a procedural mechanism for fulfilling FRAND obligations without rewriting substantive legal principles."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders can use arbitration-linked interim offers to streamline global licensing and reduce exposure to disparate national rulings.", "Practice note: Implementers must assess whether rejecting arbitration clauses in interim offers jeopardizes their ability to secure judicial determinations in preferred forums.", "What to monitor: Watch for adoption of similar arbitration-centric licensing models in other jurisdictions and subsequent challenges to their enforceability."]
core_points_en: UK Court of Appeal recognized adjustable license offers with ICC arbitration as objectively RAND-compliant proposals.
Implementers refusing such arbitration-based offers may lose access to English court declaratory jurisdiction for rate setting.
Judgment validates arbitration as a procedural mechanism for fulfilling FRAND obligations without rewriting substantive legal principles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders can use arbitration-linked interim offers to streamline global licensing and reduce exposure to disparate national rulings.
Practice note: Implementers must assess whether rejecting arbitration clauses in interim offers jeopardizes their ability to secure judicial determinations in preferred forums.
What to monitor: Watch for adoption of similar arbitration-centric licensing models in other jurisdictions and subsequent challenges to their enforceability.

items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Dominant Position in FRAND Context and Implementer's Willingness
items.summary: The Düsseldorf Local Division held that a dominant position within the meaning of Article 102 TFEU may arise if smart TVs complying with a common standard...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方法院在 Dolby 诉 Beko 案中裁定，若符合标准的智能电视无许可无法上市，专利权人可能拥有市场支配地位。法院强调，若实施者未在 FRAND 谈判初期表明许可意愿，则无需审查专利权人报价是否合规。该判决遵循华为诉中兴案框架，并与曼海姆分院立场保持一致。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Düsseldorf Local Division ruled that a dominant position under Article 102 TFEU may exist if standard-compliant smart TVs cannot be marketed without a license for the patent in suit, given consumer expectations for common codecs. The Court held that if an implementer fails to indicate willingness to conclude a license during initial FRAND negotiations, the patentee’s offer need not be examined for FRAND compliance. This decision aligns with the Mannheim Local Division’s approach in Panasonic v Oppo regarding the Huawei v ZTE framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧出口智能电视等标准产品时，须在收到侵权通知后立即明确表达许可意愿，否则将丧失 FRAND 抗辩机会。建议提前建立标准化应对流程，避免因程序瑕疵导致败诉风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies the threshold for establishing dominance in SEP cases involving consumer electronics and reinforces strict procedural requirements for implementers. Rights holders can rely on this to streamline enforcement against unresponsive parties, while implementers must ensure immediate, explicit responses to infringement notices to preserve FRAND defenses.

items.title: The UPC And License Agreements
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院与专利许可协议
items.title_en: UPC Impact on License Agreements: Opt-Out Rights and Unitary Patent Decisions
items.summary: 2023 will most likely be the year the Unified Patent Court (UPC) will open its doors. With the imminent start of the new European court system, the underlying legal framework will also become applicable.
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着统一专利法院（UPC）即将启用，其法律框架将自动适用于传统欧洲专利，除非权利人声明退出。文章指出，UPC的集中执行机制伴随集中无效风险，需审查现有及未来许可协议中关于退出机制、申请单一效力专利及执行权的条款，明确专利权人与被许可人的决策权限，以确保与新系统兼容。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) introduces centralized enforcement alongside risks of centralized validity attacks. Classic European patents fall under UPC jurisdiction automatically unless an opt-out is declared. This analysis examines how the new framework influences license agreement drafting, particularly regarding who holds the authority to decide on opt-outs or applications for Unitary Patents. While patentees typically maintain patents, licensees may control maintenance or enforcement, complicating decision-making rights. The article advises reviewing existing contracts to evaluate party interests and recommends including explicit rules in future agreements to clarify whether the licensee or patentee decides on opting out or entering the unitary system.
items.ai_insight_zh: UPC的引入改变了专利执行与无效的风险格局，许可协议中的权利分配条款需重新评估。实务中应关注合同是否明确约定了退出机制、单一专利申请及诉讼执行的决策主体，以应对新司法体系下的不确定性。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders must align license terms with UPC procedural requirements to prevent strategic deadlocks. Clarifying decision-making authority for opt-outs and unitary patent conversions is essential for effective portfolio management and litigation readiness under the new European judicial framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC启动后，传统欧洲专利自动纳入管辖，需主动声明退出以避免集中无效风险", "审查许可协议需明确谁有权决定退出UPC管辖，通常取决于维护义务或控制权归属", "协议应规定由谁决定是否申请具有单一效力的欧洲专利，以及在新旧体系下的执行权归属"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Classic European patents automatically enter the UPC system unless stakeholders actively declare an opt-out to avoid centralized validity risks.", "Opt-out decisions depend on contractual rights regarding patent maintenance and enforcement, requiring careful review of existing license terms.", "Future agreements should explicitly define which party controls opt-out declarations and applications for European Patents with Unitary Effect."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["许可协议需明确约定退出UPC管辖的决策权归属，避免因地权属不清导致策略被动", "应审查合同中关于申请单一效力专利的条款，确保与新系统的进入机制相兼容", "需关注UPC规则下专利执行权的分配，评估其对现有许可关系及诉讼策略的潜在影响"]

Opt-out decisions depend on contractual rights regarding patent maintenance and enforcement, requiring careful review of existing license terms.

items.title: LD Mannheim, April 20, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1291/2026, Anti-Anti Suit Injunction Against “Interim Licence” Application Before Chinese Court
items.title_zh: 曼海姆地院就UPC案发布针对中国临时许可申请的反反诉禁令
items.title_en: UPC Mannheim Issues Anti-Anti-Suit Injunction Against Chinese Court Interim Licence
items.summary: The UPC has jurisdiction to issue an AASI to secure pending patent infringement proceedings before the UPC (Art. 31, 32(1)(c), 33(1)(a) UPCA) (mn. 16 et seq.)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）有权签发反反诉禁令以保全 pending 侵权诉讼。全球“临时许可”若旨在阻止专利权人在外国（含UPC）行使权利，实质构成反诉禁令。曼海姆地院认定此类措施违反国际民事诉讼基本原则，且非保护中国费率设定所必需，故支持签发禁令以维护UPC管辖权及专利执行权。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Local Division in Mannheim ruled that it possesses jurisdiction to issue an anti-anti-suit injunction (AASI) to protect pending patent infringement proceedings under Articles 31, 32(1)(c), and 33(1)(a) of the UPC Agreement. The Court determined that applying for a foreign injunction to prevent a patentee from enforcing rights within UPC Contracting States constitutes imminent infringement. Consequently, a worldwide 'Interim Licence' granted by a Chinese court was characterized as a de facto anti-suit injunction designed to block injunctive relief in foreign forums, including the UPC. This ruling reinforces the UPC’s authority to secure its judicial remedies against parallel foreign proceedings that undermine its jurisdiction in standard-essential patent disputes.
items.ai_insight_en: This decision signals that reliance on foreign rate-setting orders or interim licences to halt UPC litigation is increasingly ineffective. Parties involved in cross-border SEP disputes should reassess strategies that directly challenge UPC jurisdiction through parallel foreign proceedings. Instead, focusing on robust FRAND compliance defenses and engaging in good-faith settlement negotiations may offer more viable pathways to resolve conflicts. Ignoring this shift risks severe financial sanctions and potential market access restrictions due to non-compliance with UPC orders, necessitating a recalibration of global litigation tactics.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC依据UPCA第31、32条有权签发反反诉禁令，以保全pending侵权诉讼中的专…", "旨在阻止专利权人在UPC等外国论坛寻求禁令的全球“临时许可”，实质构成反诉禁令", "平行向中国法院申请反反诉禁令不产生未决诉讼效力，因涉及不同知识产权主张"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC has jurisdiction to issue an AASI to safeguard pending infringement actions under Articles 31, 32, and 33 UPCA.", "Foreign applications aiming to block UPC enforcement constitute imminent infringement justifying interim relief measures.", "A Chinese global interim licence is substantively an anti-suit injunction violating international civil procedure principles."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Global SEP holders must recognize that foreign interim licences will not automatically stay UPC infringement proceedings.", "Practice note: Avoid direct jurisdictional confrontations via foreign courts; prioritize substantive FRAND arguments within the UPC framework.", "What to monitor: Enforcement mechanisms for UPC AASIs and potential financial penalties for non-compliance with such orders."]
core_points_en: The UPC has jurisdiction to issue an AASI to safeguard pending infringement actions under Articles 31, 32, and 33 UPCA.
Foreign applications aiming to block UPC enforcement constitute imminent infringement justifying interim relief measures.
A Chinese global interim licence is substantively an anti-suit injunction violating international civil procedure principles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Global SEP holders must recognize that foreign interim licences will not automatically stay UPC infringement proceedings.
Practice note: Avoid direct jurisdictional confrontations via foreign courts; prioritize substantive FRAND arguments within the UPC framework.
What to monitor: Enforcement mechanisms for UPC AASIs and potential financial penalties for non-compliance with such orders.

items.title: LD Paris, April 10, 2026, Procedural Order, UPC_CFI_301/2025
items.title_zh: 巴黎地院程序令：FRAND抗辩中原告享有最后陈述权
items.title_en: UPC Paris: Claimant Entitled to Final Submission in FRAND Defence Proceedings
items.summary: Unlike in case of a counterclaim for revocation (R. 29 et seq. RoP) or an application to amend the patent (R. 30 et seq. RoP), where the number and content of the pleadings are precisely set out, the Rules of Procedure do not contain respective provisions with respect to a FRAND defence.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院巴黎地方分院在Orange诉HMD Global案（UPC_CFI_301/2025）中发布程序令。鉴于《程序规则》未明确规定FRAND抗辩的诉状数量与内容，法院依据公平原则及对审原则裁定：为确保公平审判，作为FRAND抗辩反诉被告的原告有权进行最终陈述，被告不得再行回应。
items.ai_summary_en: In Orange SA v. HMD Global Oy, the UPC Local Division in Paris issued a procedural order addressing the absence of specific pleading rules for FRAND defences under the Rules of Procedure. Unlike provisions for revocation counterclaims or patent amendments, the Rules do not prescribe the number or content of pleadings for FRAND issues. Relying on general principles of fairness and adversarial proceedings from the Preamble, the Court held that the claimant, acting as counter-defendant to the FRAND defence, is entitled to the final word to ensure a fair trial. This ruling permits the claimant’s final submission while denying the defendant’s request for further response, without assessing whether the FRAND arguments were new. This decision clarifies procedural expectations and fills a gap in the current framework.
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling establishes a significant procedural dynamic where the party asserting infringement retains the final say on FRAND issues. For defendants raising FRAND defences, this creates a strategic imperative to present comprehensive arguments and evidence early in the proceedings. Reliance on subsequent rebuttals is no longer viable. Legal teams must prioritize thorough preparation for initial and intermediate stages to mitigate the disadvantage of being unable to respond to the claimant’s final submissions. Proactive case management is essential to lock in key factual and legal positions before the claimant exercises their right to the last word.

items.title_zh: UPC上诉庭澄清初步异议处理权限归属
items.title_en: UPC CoA: Panel May Defer Preliminary Objections to Main Proceedings, Not Just Judge-Rapporteur
items.summary: The Court of Appeal clarifies that a decision under R. 20.2 RoP to deal with a preliminary objection in the main proceedings is not reserved to the judge-rapporteur alone.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在Vivo与Sun Patent Trust案中裁定，根据《程序规则》第20.2条，将初步异议移交主诉讼程序审理的决定权并非独属于报告法官。合议庭亦有权决定将异议与实体 merits 一并审理。上诉庭确认一审法院拥有广泛的案件管理裁量权，巴黎地方分院将FRAND相关请求与侵权及禁令诉求合并审理并未越权。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal clarified that under Rule 20.2 of the Rules of Procedure, the decision to defer a preliminary objection to the main proceedings is not reserved exclusively to the judge-rapporteur. The panel itself may determine that the objection should be addressed alongside the merits. This ruling confirms that the Court of First Instance possesses broad case-management discretion to decide whether to rule on preliminary objections promptly or defer them, depending on procedural appropriateness. Consequently, appellate review of such case-management decisions is limited. In this specific FRAND-related patent dispute, the Paris Local Division was entitled to treat the admissibility issue as sufficiently linked to the infringement action and injunction request, allowing it to be resolved within the main proceedings rather than as a standalone preliminary matter.
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment underscores the significant case-management discretion afforded to UPC divisions, particularly regarding the timing of preliminary objection rulings. Parties facing SEP litigation must anticipate that tribunals may merge jurisdictional or validity challenges with substantive merits, especially where FRAND defenses are implicated. Litigants should prepare comprehensive strategies that address both procedural admissibility and substantive infringement simultaneously, rather than relying on early dismissal opportunities. Understanding how different local divisions exercise this discretion is critical for effective litigation planning and resource allocation in European patent disputes.

items.ai_summary_en: Maiwald partner Dr. Derk Vos contributes to the Corporate Disputes Magazine Worldwatch section on patent litigation. The analysis surveys enforcement dynamics across China, the US, UK, Netherlands, and Germany. It examines landmark decisions affecting validity and infringement interpretations, including jurisdictional questions and FRAND issues. Key topics include cost and timeline drivers such as Germany’s bifurcation system and the resulting injunction gap, alongside strategic implications under the Unified Patent Court (UPC). The discussion also addresses the evolving SEP/FRAND landscape, the impact of AI-enabled prior-art search on validity attacks, and practical recommendations for aligning litigation preparation with business objectives across national courts, the EPO, and the UPC.
items.ai_insight_en: This comparative overview highlights critical divergences in European patent enforcement, particularly between national systems like Germany’s bifurcated model and the centralized UPC framework. For rights holders, understanding these procedural differences is essential for effective forum selection and risk management in SEP/FRAND disputes. The integration of AI tools in validity challenges further necessitates updated due diligence protocols. Practitioners should note how recent case law influences the interaction between infringement and nullity actions, shaping broader enforcement strategies across multiple jurisdictions.

items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月11日，英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布2026-2027企业计划，明确将标准必要专利（SEP）作为政策重点。针对当前生态系统存在的许可不透明、必要性不确定及争议解决成本高企等结构性挑战，UKIPO计划在2026年完成政策制定并推动监管框架变革。此举旨在提升英国创新吸引力，虽无即时立法，但预示未来将在许可透明度、SEP必要性认定及纠纷效率方面进行改革。
items.ai_summary_en: On 11 May 2026, the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) published its Corporate Plan for 2026–2027, identifying standard essential patents (SEPs) as a critical policy priority. The plan acknowledges longstanding structural challenges within the SEP ecosystem, specifically limited licensing transparency, uncertainty regarding patent essentiality, and costly dispute resolution mechanisms that disproportionately impact small and medium-sized enterprises. While no immediate legislative changes are introduced, the UKIPO intends to complete policy development in 2026, aiming to enhance regulatory frameworks to support the UK’s position as a leading jurisdiction for innovation and technology-driven industries.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国通信及科技企业在英面临更严格的SEP合规环境。建议密切关注UKIPO后续政策细则，提前评估现有许可协议的透明度与必要性条款风险。应加强内部SEP管理能力，积极参与行业咨询，以应对潜在的监管变化及更高的争议解决效率要求，避免在新一轮改革中处于被动地位。
items.ai_insight_en: This update signals a sustained regulatory focus on SEP governance in the UK, aligning with broader European efforts to streamline SEP licensing and enforcement. For rights holders and implementers, the emphasis on reducing complexity and cost suggests future frameworks may prioritize procedural efficiency and clarity over substantive FRAND rate-setting. Stakeholders should prepare for potential shifts in how essentiality is verified and how disputes are mediated, ensuring internal compliance structures are adaptable to upcoming policy refinements aimed at supporting SMEs and technological growth.

items.summary: In two separate decisions handed down on the same day, the Court of Appeal of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) imposed a licensing bar on...
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院在Sun Patent诉Vivo及爱立信诉华硕两案中，拒绝原告提出的“仅限外部人员”保密机制，转而裁定对接触机密信息的被告员工实施一定期限的许可谈判禁令。法院认为员工身份不足以剥夺其访问权，但可通过禁止参与特定第三方许可谈判来减轻潜在损害，以此平衡公平审判权与第三方利益保护。
items.ai_summary_en: In two separate decisions issued on the same day, the Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal rejected claimants' requests for an external-eyes-only confidentiality regime in standard essential patent disputes involving Sun Patent Trust v Vivo and Ericsson v ASUS. The Court prioritized defendants' right to effective remedies and fair trial access over broad exclusion of employees. Instead, it upheld a licensing bar prohibiting specific named employees from participating in third-party licensing negotiations for a defined period. This approach balances trade secret protection with procedural fairness, establishing a new precedent for handling confidential information in FRAND litigation at the UPC.
items.ai_insight_en: Litigants in European standard essential patent disputes should anticipate that courts will generally grant defendant employees access to core licensing data rather than imposing total isolation. Parties must proactively establish internal compliance mechanisms, including strict firewalls, to ensure designated personnel do not participate in relevant licensing negotiations. Violating these court-ordered bars may lead to adverse legal presumptions or sanctions. Consequently, strategic planning should focus on managing internal information flows and selecting appropriate non-negotiating staff for case coordination to mitigate regulatory risks while leveraging broader evidence disclosure opportunities.

items.title: UPC Court of Appeal Clarifies Limits of International Jurisdiction – Keeex v Adobe | EIP
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院澄清国际管辖权界限：Keeex诉Adobe案
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Limits International Jurisdiction in Keeex v Adobe
items.summary: The UPC Court of Appeal rules that Article 7(2) Brussels Regulation jurisdiction cannot extend to alleged infringement outside UPCA territory, overturning the Paris Local Division.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC上诉法院裁定，依据《布鲁塞尔条例》第7(2)条的损害发生地管辖权，不能延伸至统一专利法院协定领土之外的侵权行为。该案中，巴黎地方分院此前认定对瑞士、英国等非UPC成员国的侵权拥有管辖权，但上诉法院推翻了这一决定，明确管辖权限于UPC领土范围内。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal ruled that Article 7(2) of the Brussels Regulation cannot extend jurisdiction to alleged infringements occurring outside UPCA territories. This decision overturns the Paris Local Division, which had previously accepted jurisdiction over acts in non-UPCA states like Switzerland and the UK. The case involved defendants domiciled primarily in the US and Ireland, with claims covering multiple European jurisdictions. The Court clarified that while internal jurisdiction exists for infringements within UPCA territory, the place of harm basis does not validate authority over territories not party to the Unified Patent Court Agreement.
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies the territorial limits of UPC jurisdiction, preventing rights holders from using a single UPC action to cover non-member states. Litigants must now separate claims for UPCA and non-UPCA territories, likely increasing the need for parallel proceedings in national courts for countries like the UK and Switzerland.

items.ai_summary_zh: 原文仅提供标题及“2026年第14周”标识，正文内容缺失。标题显示统一专利法院（UPC）上诉院正在处理或已就“在临时禁令程序中是否可以修改专利”这一程序性问题作出裁决或进行审理。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines a key procedural issue addressed by the UPC Court of Appeal regarding whether patent holders may amend their patents during preliminary injunction proceedings. Although the Unified Patent Court Rules permit such amendments, courts must carefully balance the need for litigation efficiency against the defendant's right to a fair defense. The article highlights that if proposed amendments alter the subject matter of the dispute or cause significant delays, the court is likely to reject them to maintain procedural speed. This guidance offers critical strategic insights for parties involved in UPC litigation, emphasizing the importance of timing and procedural conduct when seeking or opposing amendments in urgent interim relief contexts.
items.ai_insight_en: Parties facing UPC preliminary injunctions should anticipate potential strategies where opponents seek patent amendments to circumvent invalidity defenses. It is advisable to prepare diverse combinations of non-infringement and invalidity evidence in advance. Stakeholders must closely monitor procedural milestones and promptly raise objections to protect their interests. By proactively addressing these risks, rights holders and defendants can avoid being placed in a passive position due to unexpected amendments, ensuring robust protection of their legal rights throughout the expedited proceedings.

items.title: Regional Court Munich puts decision in ZTE vs Samsung on hold
items.title_zh: 慕尼黑地院中止 ZTE 诉三星案以待无效裁决
items.title_en: Munich Regional Court Suspends ZTE v Samsung SEP Infringement Case Pending Validity Ruling
items.summary: The Regional Court Munich has handed down another decision in the complex dispute between ZTE and Samsung over SEPs.
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地区法院在 ZTE 与三星的 4G/5G 标准必要专利纠纷中，因联邦专利法院对涉案专利有效性存疑，决定中止侵权诉讼程序。该案原被视为检验慕尼黑法院不同审判庭对 FRAND 抗辩新立场的风向标，现需等待 2027 年无效审理结果，导致案件显著延期。
items.ai_summary_en: The Munich Regional Court has suspended infringement proceedings in the ongoing standard-essential patent dispute between ZTE and Samsung. The decision follows a preliminary opinion from the Federal Patent Court expressing doubts regarding the validity of ZTE’s patent EP 2 375 827. Consequently, the infringement suit is stayed until the nullity action is resolved, with hearings scheduled for mid-2027. This case was closely watched as a potential indicator of how different chambers within the Munich court would align on recent FRAND defense approaches established by another chamber. The suspension highlights the continued impact of Germany’s bifurcated system, where validity challenges can significantly delay infringement judgments. Both parties are engaged in eight parallel actions across the Unified Patent Court and German courts concerning 4G and 5G technologies.
items.ai_insight_en: In Germany’s bifurcated patent system, initiating parallel nullity proceedings remains a critical strategy for defendants facing SEP infringement claims. By challenging validity early, rights holders or alleged infringers can secure stays that prevent immediate injunctions, thereby buying time for licensing negotiations or technical workarounds. However, stakeholders must anticipate prolonged uncertainty and increased legal costs associated with extended timelines. Effective management requires robust financial planning and proactive engagement in FRAND discussions during these delays. Monitoring how different judicial chambers interpret FRAND obligations alongside validity challenges will be essential for predicting case trajectories and managing portfolio risks effectively.

items.title: On the way to a global FRAND rate: Nokia drops claims against Warner and Paramount
items.title_zh: 迈向全球FRAND费率：诺基亚撤回对华纳及派拉蒙的索赔
items.title_en: Nokia Withdraws UPC and Munich Claims Against Warner and Paramount After UK FRAND Agreement
items.summary: After agreeing to UK FRAND rate setting, Nokia has withdrawn its pending actions against Warner Bros. and Paramount at the UPC and in Munich.
items.ai_summary_zh: 诺基亚在与华纳兄弟和派拉蒙同意由英国法院设定全球FRAND费率后，已撤回在统一专利法院（UPC）及慕尼黑地区法院的未决诉讼。UPC登记显示，涉及EP 4 250 732的案件已撤销；慕尼黑法院确认涉及EP 4 099 700和EP 2 130 150的诉讼亦被撤回。目前，关于H.264/H.265视频相关标准必要专利的全球许可条款确定程序仍在英国高等法院进行。
items.ai_summary_en: Nokia has withdrawn pending patent infringement actions against Warner Bros. and Paramount at the Unified Patent Court (UPC) and the Munich Regional Court. This procedural shift follows an agreement for the UK High Court to determine global FRAND licensing terms for Nokia’s video coding standard-essential patents. The UPC proceedings concerned EP 4 250 732, while the Munich cases involved EP 4 099 700 and EP 2 130 150. All three patents relate to H.264 and H.265 video compression technologies. Although the European litigation has ceased, the substantive determination of interim and final global license rates continues exclusively within the UK jurisdiction, consolidating the dispute resolution process.
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights the strategic preference for consolidating global SEP rate-setting in a single jurisdiction to avoid conflicting rulings. By accepting UK court authority, parties streamline enforcement across Europe. Rights holders should note how UPC proceedings can be efficiently stayed or withdrawn when parallel global rate determinations are secured elsewhere, reducing multi-jurisdictional litigation costs.

items.title: Local division Mannheim flirts with setting FRAND rate
items.title_zh: UPC 曼海姆分部倾向裁定 FRAND 费率
items.title_en: UPC Mannheim Division Signals Openness to Determining FRAND Rates in ZTE v Samsung
items.summary: Following Munich Regional Court, Mannheim local division is now also opening up to making a FRAND-rate determination.
items.ai_summary_zh: 继慕尼黑地方法院后，统一专利法院（UPC）曼海姆地方分院在 ZTE 诉三星案中展现新动向。主审法官托赫特曼提出 UPC 框架下裁定 FRAND 费率的程序构想，打破德国法院长期回避此议题的传统。因三星提出反诉，法庭需对此作出裁决，预示欧洲标准必要专利诉讼格局生变。
items.ai_summary_en: Following the Munich Regional Court, the Unified Patent Court’s Mannheim Local Division is shifting away from the traditional German judicial reluctance to determine Fair, Reasonable, and Non-Discriminatory (FRAND) royalty rates. In the ongoing ZTE v Samsung proceedings, Presiding Judge Peter Tochtermann outlined a procedural framework for such determinations within the UPC system. This development arises because Samsung filed a FRAND counterclaim, compelling the court to address the issue substantively rather than avoiding it. The hearing attracted significant attention from legal and economic experts across Europe and Asia. This move suggests a potential transformation in European standard-essential patent litigation, challenging the historical dominance of UK courts in setting global FRAND terms and establishing new precedents for continental European jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in standard-essential patent disputes should anticipate increased judicial willingness in Germany to set binding FRAND rates. Rights holders and implementers must adapt their Unified Patent Court litigation strategies by preparing robust economic evidence and valuation models to support or challenge proposed royalty figures. The interplay between the Mannheim and Paris divisions may offer strategic opportunities for forum selection. Parties should closely monitor how these new procedural frameworks develop, as they could significantly influence licensing negotiations and the overall landscape of SEP enforcement in Europe, potentially reducing reliance on non-European jurisdictions for rate-setting decisions.
